summary:
Barium enemas, especially with double contrast, have lower sensitivity than colonoscopy and perform poorly in detecting small polyps. Therefore, even a second review fails to improve detection rates enough to justify continued reliance on this method over better alternatives such as colonoscopy or CT colonography. 

---

(End of Round)
